workers.codex: OpenAI Codex CLI v0.125.0, model gpt-5.5
workers.copilot: GitHub Copilot CLI, resolved model claude-fable-5 (per ~/.copilot config)
workers.kimi: Kimi CLI, model K2.7 Code (per user session)

graders: same five CLIs, blind, per-grader shuffled candidate letters; per-grader letter mappings held outside the repo until unblinding

worker_verdicts.fable-conductor: rework required (grades/fable-review.md, written before reading worker outputs)

rank_matrix_note: 4 of 5 graders produced the identical model ranking despite different letter shuffles; only copilot-gpt54 diverged (kimi 2nd, grok 4th, itself 5th).

observations.position_bias: none observed — different shuffles per grader, same model ranking from 4/5
observations.self_preference: none — copilot-gpt54 and kimi ranked themselves lower than peers did; copilot ranked itself 1st but so did all four other graders
observations.verbosity_caveat: rank order closely tracks cleaned output length (copilot 11.6KB ranked 1st, copilot-gpt54 3.4KB near-last); thoroughness vs length not separable at n=1 — human anchor needed

adapter_quirks_learned.codex: reads stdin when piped — close stdin ($null |) or it hangs; needs --skip-git-repo-check outside a git repo; reasoning streams to stderr
adapter_quirks_learned.kimi: Python app — needs PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 when stdout is redirected on Windows (cp1252 dies on non-ASCII); --print emits an event stream (extract last TextPart); log rotation collides with concurrent kimi processes (non-fatal)
adapter_quirks_learned.copilot: -p is headless; output includes tool-call transcript furniture that must be stripped; --model selects hosted model (gpt-5.4 verified)
adapter_quirks_learned.gemini: CLI dead for individual accounts (IneligibleTierError, migrate-to-Antigravity); works only with GEMINI_API_KEY direct auth
adapter_quirks_learned.grok: -p/--single is single-turn headless; prepends brief prose preamble before markdown

contamination_log: conductor skimmed the first ~25 lines of grok's review during dispatch monitoring, before writing fable-review.md (findings 1-2 were noted prior to that skim)
peer grades were unblinded to the conductor before the human grading pass; human instructed to grade from anonymized/ before opening grades/

human_grading.result: human ranking matches peer consensus except one adjacent swap (codex/grok); spearman 0.90, kendall tau 0.80
human_grading.verbosity_confound_update: human anchor independently ranked the longest review 1st and confirmed its unique findings are real — for this task, length tracked genuine thoroughness, not grader bias

prompt_steering_note: TASK.md deliverable item 3 ('consider what data the caller in collectManifest actually passes') was written after the conductor had already found the starved-ranking bug — it leaked a prior and partially steered workers toward finding 1. The codePointAt tie-break bug, the mirror divergences, and all unique copilot findings were NOT hinted and were found independently. Lesson for task-002: author the task spec before the conductor forms its own review, or strip analytical hints from deliverable questions.
